name: Red Sea Crisis (2024)
description: 3 cables (AAE-1, EIG, SEACOM/TGN-Eurasia) cut in the Red Sea, February 2024. 25% of Asia-Europe data traffic disrupted.

name: West Africa Cuts (2024)
description: 4 cables (WACS, MainOne, SAT-3, ACE) severed off Abidjan at the Le Trou Sans Fond submarine canyon on March 14, 2024. 13 African countries impacted.

name: Baltic Sea Sabotage (2024)
description: BCS East-West Interlink and C-Lion1 cables damaged November 17-18, 2024. Chinese vessel Yi Peng 3 suspected. High terrestrial redundancy limited impact.

name: Luzon Strait Earthquake (2006)
description: M7.0 Hengchun earthquake on December 26, 2006 triggered submarine landslides severing 8-22 cables in the Luzon Strait. Internet disrupted across Asia for weeks.

name: Mediterranean Cuts (2008)
description: SEA-ME-WE 4 and FLAG cables cut near Alexandria, Egypt on January 30, 2008. 70% disruption in Egypt, 60% in India, affecting 14 countries.

name: Vietnam Cable Failures (2023)
description: 4 of 5 international cables (AAG, AAE-1, APG, TGN-IA; SMW-3 retired/not in dataset) connecting Vietnam were damaged or degraded. 75% of international capacity lost.
